_why: Declares which Open WebUI capability flags to enable for AI-OS Nemotron Ultra. Each flag is justified.

vision._why: Nemotron-3-Ultra-550B is a text model. Enabling vision in OWU would route image inputs to a capability the model cannot satisfy.

file_handling._why: Users frequently upload code files, architecture diagrams (as text), and documents for analysis. File handling enriches RAG ingestion.

code_execution._why: Code execution sandbox is not available in the target Open WebUI deployment. Enabling it would create a misleading UI affordance.

web_search._why: Time-sensitive queries (current library versions, breaking changes) require web search. Governed by tool routing rules in compiled_prompt_v2.md.

tools._why: GitHub, Brain Memory, and Calculator tools are registered. Tool routing is governed by the compiled prompt to minimise RPM usage.

citations._why: Citation display is required for RAG transparency. Users must be able to verify the source of retrieved content.

memory._why: Cross-session project continuity is a core AI-OS capability. Governed by memory.json policy.

streaming._why: Streaming is critical on NIM Free Tier. It delivers first token quickly, masking the high latency of 550B inference and improving perceived responsiveness.

system_prompt_override._why: compiled_prompt_v2.md is version-controlled. Allowing per-user system prompt override would break runtime consistency and version traceability.

tool_manifest.name: Brain Memory
tool_manifest.routing_rule: only when user-specific context materially changes the answer

tool_manifest.name: Web Search
tool_manifest.routing_rule: time-sensitive facts, current versions, breaking news

tool_manifest.name: Calculator
tool_manifest.routing_rule: multi-step arithmetic, statistical derivations requiring precision
